commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the native hook relay unavailable fallback so Codex PreToolUse returns no output when no before-tool-call policy is detected, and updates CLI tests for that behavior.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests -18. Total -12 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: the PR's added branch returns empty stdout based on process-local `hasBeforeToolCallPolicy()` after the CLI can no longer query the originating relay. I did not run a live Codex native relay in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the patch changes a shipped fail-closed tool-policy fallback and the contributor proof is not inspectable; automation should not repair or merge until the policy-state direction is settled.

target.title: fix: allow missing native hook relay without policy
target.body:
## Summary
- Let unavailable native PreToolUse relays fall back to a provider no-op when no before-tool policy is configured.
- Keep fail-closed behavior for permission requests and for PreToolUse when a before-tool policy exists.
- Update native hook relay CLI tests to cover the no-policy fallback.

## Validation
- Runtime smoke: bogus missing PreToolUse relay returns exit code 0 with empty stdout.
- Runtime smoke: bogus missing PermissionRequest relay still returns a deny decision.
- Local full targeted Vitest was attempted, but dependency hydration stalled on registry package fetches in this environment.
target.author: woodym-dotcom
